title: Odicardis crista-galli
intro:
I couldn't find information on "Odicardis crista-galli."  However, "Cardiospermum halicacabum"  is a plant that matches the name "Odicardis crista-galli" - it is a flowering plant that belongs to the Sapindaceae family. 

Here is a paragraph about "Cardiospermum halicacabum":

Cardiospermum halicacabum, commonly known as the balloon vine or love-in-a-puff, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Cardiospermum halicacabum include cardiospermol, cardiospermoside, and other alkaloids and flavonoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Cardiospermum halicacabum include capsules, tinctures, and creams, which are used topically to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
